assertions.expectation: Identifies this as an admission webhook compatibility issue — the webhook is rejecting pod creation requests
assertions.reason: Response correctly identifies the root cause as cert-manager webhook certificates being incompatible with Kubernetes 1.32 API server and explains this causes admission webhook errors blocking pod creation

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify the problematic webhook (kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations / mutatingwebhookconfigurations)
assertions.reason: Response includes both kubectl get validatingwebhookconfigurations and kubectl get mutatingwebhookconfigurations commands with grep cert-manager filter to identify the specific problematic webhook

assertions.expectation: Offers an immediate fix: temporarily disable or remove the problematic webhook
assertions.reason: Response provides kubectl patch commands to set failurePolicy to 'Ignore' and a fallback option to delete webhook configurations entirely

assertions.expectation: Provides the kubectl delete command for the webhook configuration as emergency mitigation
assertions.reason: Response includes specific kubectl delete commands for both validatingwebhookconfigurations and mutatingwebhookconfigurations cert-manager-webhook

assertions.expectation: Warns that deleting the webhook removes validation — it's a temporary fix
assertions.reason: Response clearly states this is a temporary fix and includes a section on restoring webhook security after the permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking cert-manager version compatibility with Kubernetes 1.32
assertions.reason: Response includes commands to check cert-manager version and explicitly mentions that cert-manager 1.13+ supports Kubernetes 1.32

assertions.expectation: Advises upgrading cert-manager to a version that supports 1.30 as the permanent fix
assertions.reason: Response recommends upgrading to cert-manager v1.15.0 and provides both Helm and kubectl commands for the upgrade, though it targets 1.32 support rather than specifically mentioning 1.30

assertions.expectation: Mentions setting failurePolicy to Ignore as a less drastic alternative to deletion
assertions.reason: Response presents setting failurePolicy to 'Ignore' as the primary immediate mitigation and deletion as a fallback option

assertions.expectation: Includes commands to verify pods can be created after the fix
assertions.reason: Response includes kubectl run test-pod commands to verify pod creation works both after initial mitigation and after permanent fix

assertions.expectation: Notes that any webhook configuration changes (failurePolicy, cert-manager version) must be persisted in the source of truth (Helm chart, GitOps manifests, CI/CD pipeline) to survive redeployment
assertions.reason: While the response provides upgrade commands and mentions using Helm, it does not explicitly warn about persisting changes in source of truth systems like GitOps manifests or CI/CD pipelines

assertions.expectation: Qualifies failurePolicy: Ignore as a temporary debugging measure with explicit risks, recommending revert to Fail after the root cause is resolved
assertions.reason: Response explicitly shows restoring failurePolicy to 'Fail' after the permanent fix and emphasizes this is temporary mitigation while the root cause is resolved
